title: Global heap over flow in sqlite3VdbeMemPrettyPrint
description:
yongheng added on 2020-01-06 18:39:09:
Consider the following test case:
---
PRAGMA vdbe_trace=true; ; CREATE TABLE v0 ( v1 , v2 , v3 UNIQUE ) ;
CREATE VIEW v4 ( v5 ) AS SELECT v3 FROM v0 ORDER BY v3 , v3 , v3 , v2 ;
SELECT DISTINCT 9 IN ( SELECT zeroblob ( 10 * 10 * 10 ) ) ;
---
It triggers an global heap overflow in debug build with asan, for both release code and development code.
drh added on 2020-01-06 19:25:35:
Unable to reproduce.  Nevertheless, enhancements have been made to the
sqlite3VdbeMemPrettyPrint() function so that it uses safer string handling
routines.  Perhaps that will fix the problem.  I'll mark the ticket as
"Fixed" and somebody else can reopen it if they find a new test case that
still fails.
yongheng added on 2020-01-06 19:45:59:
We can reproduce this problem stably. Here is the asan trace if it helps:
---
==31757==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: global-buffer-overflow on address 0x000002511d90 at pc 0x000000b7e954 bp 0x7ffc764b0970 sp 0x7ffc764b0968
READ of size 1 at 0x000002511d90 thread T0
    #0 0xb7e953 in sqlite3VdbeMemPrettyPrint /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/sqlite3.c:84994:49
    #1 0xb7e953 in memTracePrint /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/sqlite3.c:85067
    #2 0xb7e953 in registerTrace /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/sqlite3.c:85074
    #3 0xab3049 in sqlite3VdbeExec /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/sqlite3.c:87439:3
    #4 0x7144ae in sqlite3Step /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/sqlite3.c:82929:10
    #5 0x7144ae in sqlite3_step /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/sqlite3.c:82994
    #6 0x6c00b5 in exec_prepared_stmt /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/shell.c:11387:8
    #7 0x584584 in shell_exec /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/shell.c:11692:7
    #8 0x6ca4d6 in runOneSqlLine /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/shell.c:18278:8
    #9 0x594363 in process_input /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/shell.c:18378:17
    #10 0x532ebb in main /data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/shell.c:19136:12
    #11 0x7fc8ead4582f in __libc_start_main (/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x2082f)
    #12 0x41bd08 in _start (/data/rxz226/sqlite/bld/sqlite3+0x41bd08)
---
yongheng added on 2020-01-06 20:01:21:
But the problem has been gone after the check-in.
drh added on 2020-01-06 20:04:32:
Unable to repro.
Please show the exact version of SQLite that you are using and the exact sequence of steps you are using to build the CLI.
yongheng added on 2020-01-06 20:11:41:
For development version, it is that before this check-in [69f6a7e4].

For release version, the info is:
---
SQLite version 3.30.1 2019-10-10 20:19:45
---

Built with "-fsanitize=address -DSQLITE_DEBUG=1"
drh added on 2020-01-06 20:49:36:
Reproducing for me now.  A correct fix has been checked in.
Just to confirm:  This is all in debugging logic that is omitted from
release builds.
yongheng added on 2020-01-06 22:48:07:
Thanks for the fix. Yeah, we checked it against release build. That's why we only mentioned debug build.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value BLOB, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('test1', zeroblob(1024));
CREATE VIEW test_view AS SELECT name, value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT name FROM test_view WHERE LENGTH(value) IN (SELECT LENGTH(zeroblob(2048)));
UPDATE test_table SET value = NULL WHERE id = 1;
